what_it_does: Scans a conversation archive directory, checks each JSONL conversation file against its summary and the database index, classifies problems as missing/errored summaries, outdated files, corrupted parses, or orphaned DB rows, and provides a repair routine that removes orphans and re-indexes missing/outdated conversations (regenerating summaries, embeddings, and DB rows) while leaving corrupted files for manual review.
observations: Uses synchronous fs/path operations inside loops to inspect files.
Excludes configured projects via getExcludedProjects and a Set.
Treats an errored summary sentinel file as a missing summary so repair will re-attempt summarization.
Compares file mtimeMs against the database last_indexed timestamp to detect outdated files.
Attempts to parse every conversation to detect corruption.
Dynamically imports db.js, parser.js, embeddings.js, and summarizer.js in repairIndex to avoid circular dependencies.
Logs progress every 100 conversations and a final verified count.
Closes the database after verification and after repair.
Reports corrupted files at the end of repair without attempting to fix them.
standouts: Errored summary sentinel is intentionally classified as missing, not healthy, so repair re-runs summarization.
repairIndex both deletes orphaned DB entries and re-indexes missing/outdated files in one pass.
Corruption detection is done by actually parsing every conversation during verification.
conventions: Sync fs calls for local file metadata and reads.
Dynamic imports to break circular module dependencies.
Console logging for operational progress and final status.
concerns: Outdated files are re-indexed without first deleting the existing DB rows for that file, risking duplicate exchanges.
Summary path is derived by a simple string replacement of '.jsonl' with '-summary.txt', which could mis-match if '.jsonl' appears elsewhere in the path.
Repair extracts the project name by stripping the archive directory prefix and splitting on path.sep; fragile if paths are not normalized.
Synchronous parsing/stat-ing of every file inside nested loops may become slow for large archives.
No transaction or batching around the per-exchange insert loop in repair.
notes: This is the archive integrity watchdog: it checks whether the filesystem and database still agree, and it can automatically heal most discrepancies except corrupted files.
questions: When re-indexing an outdated conversation, are existing exchanges for that file deleted first to avoid duplicates?
What does isErroredSentinel consider an error, and can it produce false positives?
How large is the archive expected to be, given the sync-in-loop approach?
Does parseConversation validate schema, or can any parse/validation error mark a file corrupted?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to verify and repair the index; the file largely matches that purpose, though the repair of outdated files may create duplicate DB rows because it does not clear prior entries for the same file.
apparent_themes: Defensive verification with manual fallback for corruption
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make the conversation archive self-maintaining: automatically detect when the on-disk files and the searchable index have drifted, fix the common cases without human intervention, and surface only unrecoverable corruption for review.
novelty: The treatment of an errored-summary sentinel as a missing summary, so the repair path naturally re-attempts failed summarizations rather than leaving them as permanent failures.
contribution: Provides the verification and repair orchestration that keeps the archive filesystem and the database index aligned, including summary regeneration, embedding generation, and orphan cleanup.
summary_for_embedding: verify.ts exports a VerificationResult type plus verifyIndex and repairIndex functions that scan conversation archives, detect missing or errored summaries, outdated or corrupted files, and orphaned database entries, then repair the index by re-indexing missing/outdated conversations and removing orphans.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to maintain consistency between on-disk conversation archives and a searchable database index by verifying state and automatically repairing most discrepancies.

symbols.does: Defines the shape of verification findings.
symbols.description: An interface describing four issue categories: missing summaries/files, orphaned database entries, files newer than their last_indexed timestamp, and files that failed to parse.

symbols.does: Scans the archive and database to classify integrity issues.
symbols.description: Iterates project directories, finds JSONL conversation files, checks for missing or errored summaries, compares file modification time against the database last_indexed value, attempts to parse each file to detect corruption, and finally flags database rows whose archivePath no longer exists on disk.

symbols.does: Fixes missing/outdated files and removes orphaned database entries.
symbols.description: Dynamically imports indexer modules to avoid circular dependencies, deletes orphaned exchanges, re-parses and re-summarizes missing and outdated conversations, generates embeddings, inserts each exchange into the database, and reports corrupted files for manual review.
